_comment: Eviction/TTL decode fixtures. Each scenario drives a Blend pool Config contract_data change through DecodeState and asserts the resulting LedgerState liveness. They pin the Soroban liveness trap: eviction is a Live=false signal and TTL-expiry is LiveUntilLedgerSeq < ledgerSeq, both distinct from a value simply being present. Pulling those signals out of the close meta (the evicted-key set / the TTL fold) is covered elsewhere; here we only assert the decoder acts on them.

scenarios.description: Live=false (the relay extract sets this from EvictedLedgerKeys) removes a previously-live pool from LedgerState.
A change whose LiveUntilLedgerSeq is below ledgerSeq is expired, so the pool is not live even though Live=true and a value is present.
ChangeType=Restored with Live=true and a fresh LiveUntilLedgerSeq re-livens a pool that had been evicted (no prior state).
